note:
今天，一名泄密者在推特上发布了一个指向文件共享服务的链接，其中包含一位匿名人士所称的英特尔皇冠上明珠的一部分：一个20 GB的机密英特尔知识产权文件夹。泄密者将此次发布称为英特尔保密的Lake平台发布；)。&#34；
这个文件夹是由一个匿名消息来源发布的，该消息来源声称很快会有更多内容发布，虽然我们不知道该文件夹内容的确切细节，但我们已经验证了它确实存在。事实上，许多文件的标题确实与泄密者发布的所谓信息列表相关：
Kabylake(Purley Platform)BIOS参考代码和示例代码+初始化代码(其中一些作为完整历史的导出git报告)。
匿名泄密者声称黑客侵入了英特尔，这些文件是在今年早些时候获得的，并补充说，这里的大部分内容以前从未在任何地方发布过，属于保密协议(NDA)或英特尔限制机密(Intel Restricted Secret)。泄密者表示，很快会有更多文件被共享，未来泄露的部分将有更多更有趣、更机密的东西。
有趣的是，推特用户还指出，如果您在版本中找到受密码保护的zips，则密码可能是&#34；intel123&34；或&#34；intel123&34；。这不是我或我的线人设置的，这就是它是如何从英特尔获得的。
这张海报鼓励下载者寻找一些英特尔源代码中提到的后门，甚至提供了一个这样的清单的示例片段，但我们不确定代码中列出的清单背后的意图。
我们已经联系了英特尔就此事发表评论，该公司告诉我们，它正在调查这一情况。我们期待着很快会有进一步的评论。
该链接在Twitter上广为流传，但最好谨慎行事--从不可信的来源下载任何文件总是有风险的。
我认为一些关于7/10纳米真实状态的内部信息会对正在提起的诉讼感兴趣。不确定它是否会被采纳，但它可以揭示谁知道什么，什么时候这些文件是不是违反了规定。
回覆。
我希望想出密码的人不会对芯片的安全负责。LOL。但真的是一次多么危险的泄密。数以万计的政府、金融和企业计算机运行在英特尔上，他们已经在安全问题上经历了一段艰难的时期。
回覆。
有趣的是，推特用户还注意到，如果您在版本中找到受密码保护的拉链，则密码可能是&#34；intel123&34；或&#34；intel123&34；。这不是我或我的线人设置的，这就是它是如何从英特尔获得的。&#34；&#34；&#34；真的吗？
回覆。

note_en:
A leaker today posted on Twitter a link to a file sharing service that contains what an anonymous source claims is a portion of Intel's crown jewels: A 20GB folder of confidential Intel intellectual property. The leaker dubbed the release the "Intel exconfidential Lake Platform Release ;)."
 The folder has been posted by an anonymous source that claims more is coming soon, and while we don't know the exact specifics of the folder's contents, we have verified that it does exist. In fact, the title of many of the documents do correlate to the list of purported information posted by the leaker:
 Kabylake (Purley Platform) BIOS Reference Code and Sample Code + Initialization code (some of it as exported git repos with full history)
   The anonymous leaker claims the hacker "breached" Intel and the files were obtained earlier this year, adding "most of the things here have NOT been published ANYWHERE before and are classified as confidential, under NDA or Intel Restricted Secret." The leaker says more files will be shared soon, and "the future parts of this leak will have even juicier and more classified stuff."
 Interestingly, the tweeter also notes "If you find password protected zips in the release the password is probably either "Intel123" or "intel123". This was not set by me or my source, this is how it was acquired from Intel."
 The poster encourages downloaders to look for mentions of 'backdoors' in some of the Intel source code, and even provides a sample clip of one such listing, but we aren't sure of the intentions behind the listings in the code.
 We've reached out to Intel for comment on the matter, and the company tells us it is investigating the situation. We expect further comment soon.
 The link is being widely distributed on Twitter, but it might be best to exercise caution – downloading any file from an untrusted source is always a risk.
      i'm thinking some internal info about the true state of 7/10 nm would be interesting to the lawsuits being filed. not sure it would be admissible but it could shed some light on who knew what and when if those docs are in the breach.

note:
新泽西州警察局正在对五人提起网络骚扰指控，这些人与6月份上传到Twitter上的一张抗议照片有关。投诉针对最初的推特用户和其他四个转发了这条消息的人，声称他们让这名警官担心他的家人的安全。
这是反骚扰法史无前例的使用，正值全国执法部门对反警察暴行激进主义的强烈反对。如果成功，这些指控将给社交媒体上的政治活动增加重大的新风险，社交媒体上的政治活动是正在进行的抗议运动中的一个关键因素。
“如果有人知道这个婊子是谁，就把他的信息发布到这条推特下面。”
纳特利警察局在7月下旬对6月26日抗议期间发布的一条推文提出了投诉。这条现已被删除的信息包括一张蒙面值班警察的照片，并要求“如果有人知道这个婊子是谁，就把他的信息放到这条推特上。”由于戴着面具，这名警官很难从照片中辨认出来，而且似乎没有任何回复显示他的身份。
最初的发帖者和转发者被控网络骚扰，这是一项四级重罪，最高可判处18个月监禁。活动人士Gegana Sziszak是转发者之一，他在上周的GoFundMe活动中透露了这一投诉。新泽西州美国公民自由联盟向The Verge证实了它的存在，Sziszak的律师艾伦·佩鲁顿(Alan Peyrouton)也是如此。The Verge还审阅了一份Sziszak的传票副本。
“我从来没有见过这样的事情，”佩鲁顿在谈到这起案件时说。“我看不出这怎么会上升到犯罪的程度。”Sziszak最初要求3000美元聘请律师，到目前为止，她已经筹集了大约8200美元。她的传票命令她在本月晚些时候出庭。
“我看不出这怎么会上升到犯罪的程度。”
该部门代表侦探彼得·桑德梅尼科(Peter Sandomenico)指控Sziszak和其他人，起诉书称他是推文中的警官。它声称，这张照片和附带的说明威胁到这名警官“在履行职责时采取行动，使桑德梅尼科侦探担心自己、家人和财产会受到伤害。”
当Sziszak发布她的筹款活动时，该帖子没有收到回复，并被转发了五次。目前还不清楚该部门是如何发现它的存在的。然而，一些部门使用自动社交媒体监控工具来跟踪从特定位置发送的所有推文。如果这样的工具被用来监视纳特利抗议活动，它很可能已经浮出了桑多梅尼科的推文。
纳特利警察局证实，他们已经对五人提出了网络骚扰投诉。“指控是由我们部门提出的。因为一件与我们的一名警官有关的事件。然而，这件事仍在调查中，这使得我无法提供任何进一步的细节。“安东尼·蒙塔纳里中尉告诉The Verge。该部门还确认了这名警官的身份。
Sziszak在GoFundMe上写道，她被传票弄得措手不及。她写道：“我没有回答，没有说任何针对这名警察的话，对他是谁也一无所知。”“这条推文的目的是找出这名警官的信息，追究他的责任。”
虽然传票中没有提到原始海报的名字，但Sziszak和她的律师证实了他的身份是凯文·阿尔法罗(Kevin Alfaro)，后者在7月份发布了另一个GoFundMe活动。(阿尔法罗没有回复通过Twitter或GoFundMe发送的消息。)。Alfaro的GoFundMe描述了6月26日纳特利争取黑人生活的抗议活动，年轻的反种族主义示威者与亲克里斯托弗·哥伦布的反抗议者对峙。这两个团体最终被路障和警察分开，尽管新泽西州的新闻12报道说没有人被捕。
竞选描述说，阿尔法罗对那些对反抗议者“非常友好”并遮盖徽章的警察感到不安，全国各地的一些警察都采取了这种做法，以躲避抗议者的抱怨。“为了找出哪个警官和骚扰我的人交朋友，我上传了一张照片。”他的推文包括一张印在桑德梅尼科面具上的“细蓝线”美国国旗的照片--这是一个象征警察团结的象征，但充满了种族主义协会。
司法部对这五名推特用户的法律论据含糊其辞。2014年新泽西州的一项法律禁止网络骚扰，当它威胁要对某人进行身体伤害或侵犯他们的财产时，或者当它涉及发送“淫秽、不雅或淫秽”的材料时-这更多地适用于持续的骚扰运动和网络跟踪的案件。第一修正案还保护了对执勤警察拍照的权利。
新泽西州的网络骚扰法关注的是威胁和“淫秽、不雅或淫秽”的内容。
惩罚性转发的先例尤其少，即使在风险较低的非刑事案件中也是如此。2018年，MSNBC主持人乔伊·里德(Joy Reid)因转发他人涉嫌诽谤的帖子而被起诉。但原告很快撤销了转发指控，只关注里德写的声明。最近的一起诉讼称，转发推文可以算作侵犯版权，但尚未解决。抗议转发可能会受到“通信体面法”(Communications Decency Act)第230条的保护，这是一部保护网络服务及其用户免受他人帖子责任的盾牌法律。
起诉书暗示，索要桑德梅尼科的“信息”是对这名警官进行骚扰或安抚的邀请。骚扰受害者起诉了鼓励追随者虐待他们的巨魔-比如蒙大拿州的房地产经纪人坦尼娅·格什(Tanya Gersh)，她去年赢得了针对新纳粹博客作者安德鲁·安格林(Andrew Anglin)的诉讼。但在安格林发表了数十篇关于格什的文章并要求他的追随者制造一场“巨魔风暴”后，格什被吓坏了几个月。纳特利警察局没有报告除了最初的推文之外有任何骚扰，这条推文本身也没有呼吁采取任何行动，只是确认桑德梅尼科的身份。
“这似乎是一名警官在展示他起诉某人的能力。”
美国公民自由联盟-新泽西州最高法院倡导主任亚历山大·沙洛姆(Alexander Shalom)表示，这些指控是似是而非的。“(法律)旨在防止贪腐伤害，而不是过敏症和伤害感情，”他告诉The Verge。“这似乎是一名警官展示了他起诉某人的能力，尽管他无法确定犯罪要素。”
起诉点击转发按钮的人可能会把社交媒体变成雷区。即使只有很小的投诉风险，它也会阻止用户参与内容，特别是那些不确定什么构成骚扰或其他违法行为的用户。而且，对每一个转发了哪怕是一条受欢迎的帖子的人收取费用也几乎是不可能的，这将把它变成一根大棒，让强大的机构-包括警方-可以有选择地用来对付批评者。“如果它被转发了5万次呢？你要去找那5万个转发者吗？“。佩鲁顿问道。“这对我来说是不可思议的。”
无论结果如何，重罪指控都会给被告带来严重的后果。在收到投诉后的几天里，Sziszak被迫管理聘请律师的费用，以及在被冠状病毒大流行颠覆的法律体系中导航的挫折感。即使这些指控不会导致判决，提交的文件也可能会恐吓那些想要合法记录警察的抗议者，或者让其他人不敢与他们接触。
Szisznak写道：“我无法解释我对警察局的恐惧和对我的担忧。”“我担心我可能因为一些我认为正在行使我的第一修正案权利的事情而损害了我的整个未来。”
note_en:
A New Jersey police department is pursuing cyber harassment charges against five people in connection with a protest photo uploaded to Twitter in June. Complaints were served against the original tweeter and four other people who retweeted the message, alleging that they caused the officer to fear for the safety of his family.
 It’s an unprecedented use of anti-harassment laws, coming amid a nationwide law enforcement backlash against anti-police brutality activism. If successful, the charges would add significant new risks to political activity on social media, a key element in the ongoing protest movements.
 “If anyone knows who this bitch is throw his info under this tweet.”
 The Nutley Police Department filed its complaints in late July over a tweet posted during a June 26th protest. The  now-deleted message included a photo of a masked on-duty police officer with a request that “If anyone knows who this bitch is throw his info under this tweet.” Because of the mask, the officer is not readily identifiable from the photograph, and there do not appear to be any replies revealing his identity.
 The original poster and the retweeters are charged with cyber harassment, a fourth-degree felony punishable by up to 18 months in jail. Activist Georgana Sziszak, one of the retweeters, revealed the complaint  in a GoFundMe campaign last week. The American Civil Liberties Union of New Jersey confirmed its existence to  The Verge,  as did Sziszak’s attorney Alan Peyrouton.  The Verge has also reviewed a copy of Sziszak’s summons.
 “I’ve never seen anything like it,” says Peyrouton of the case. “I don’t see how that rises to the level of a crime.” Sziszak originally requested $3,000 to hire an attorney; so far, she’s raised around $8,200. Her summons orders her to appear in court later this month.
 “I don’t see how that rises to the level of a crime.”
 The department charged Sziszak and others on behalf of Detective Peter Sandomenico, who the complaint identifies as the officer in the tweet. It alleges that the photo and accompanying caption threatened the officer “acting in the performance of his duties, causing Detective Sandomenico to fear that harm will come to himself, family, and property.”
 At the time Sziszak posted her fundraiser, the post had no replies and five retweets. It’s unclear how the department discovered its existence. However, some departments use  automated social media surveillance tools to track all the tweets sent from a particular location. Had such a tool been used to surveil the Nutley protests, it would likely have surfaced the Sandomenico tweet.
 The Nutley Police Department confirmed that it had filed complaints against five people for cyber harassment. “Charges were filed by our department. For an incident relating to one of our officers. However this remains under investigation which prevents me from providing any further details,” Detective Lieutenant Anthony Montanari told  The Verge. The department also confirmed the officer’s identity.
 Sziszak wrote on GoFundMe that she had been blindsided by the summons. “I did not reply, did not say anything against this cop, and had zero clue to who he was,” she wrote. “The purpose of this tweet was to find out the officer’s information, to hold him accountable.”
  While the original poster is not named in the summons, Sziszak and her attorney corroborated his identity as Kevin Alfaro, who posted a  separate GoFundMe campaign in July. (Alfaro did not respond to messages via Twitter or GoFundMe.) Alfaro’s GoFundMe describes  a June 26th Nutley For Black Lives protest where young anti-racism demonstrators were  confronted by pro-Christopher Columbus counter-protestors. The groups were eventually separated by barricades and police, although  News 12 New Jersey reported that no arrests were made.
 The campaign description says Alfaro was upset by officers who were “very friendly” with counter-protesters and covered their badges, a practice that  some officers across the country have adopted to dodge complaints from protesters. “In an attempt to identify a specific police officer who was befriending someone harassing me, I uploaded a photo.” His tweet includes a picture of the “Thin Blue Line” American flag printed on Sandomenico’s mask — a symbol that’s used to signify police solidarity but is  fraught with racist associations.
 The department’s legal argument against the five Twitter users is murky. A 2014 New Jersey law bans online harassment when it threatens someone with physical harm or crimes against their property, or when it involves sending “lewd, indecent, or obscene” material — it’s more typically applied in cases involving persistent harassment campaigns and cyberstalking. The First Amendment also  protects the right to photograph on-duty police officers.
 New Jersey’s cyber harassment law focuses on threats and “lewd, indecent, or obscene” material
 There’s particularly little precedent for punishing retweets, even in lower-stakes non-criminal cases. MSNBC host Joy Reid  was sued in 2018 for retweeting someone else’s allegedly defamatory post. But the plaintiff  quickly dropped the retweet accusation, focusing only on statements Reid wrote.  A recent suit claims retweets can count as copyright infringement, but it’s not yet resolved. The protest retweets could potentially be covered by Section 230 of the Communications Decency Act, a shield law that protects web services  and their users from liability over other people’s posts.
 The complaint implies that requesting Sandomenico’s “info” was an invitation to harass or doxx the officer. Harassment victims  have sued trolls who encouraged followers to abuse them — like Montana real estate agent Tanya Gersh, who  won a case against neo-Nazi blogger Andrew Anglin last year. But Gersh was terrorized for months after Anglin published dozens of articles about her and asked his followers to create a “troll storm.” The Nutley Police Department did not report any harassment beyond the original tweet, which itself didn’t call for any action beyond identifying Sandomenico.
 “What this appears to be is a police officer flexing his ability to charge someone.”
 Alexander Shalom, ACLU-NJ’s director of Supreme Court advocacy, declared the charges specious. “[The law] is designed to prevent venal harm, not hypersensitivity and hurt feelings,” he told  The Verge. “What this appears to be is a police officer flexing his ability to charge someone notwithstanding the fact that he cannot establish the elements of the crime.”
 Prosecuting people for clicking the retweet button could turn social media into a minefield. It would discourage users from engaging with content if there’s even a small risk of getting a complaint, especially users who aren’t sure what constitutes harassment or other legal violations. And charging everyone who retweeted even a single popular post would be almost impossible, turning it into a cudgel that powerful institutions — including the police — could selectively use against critics. “What if it had gotten retweeted 50,000 times? Are you going to go out to those 50,000 retweeters?” asks Peyrouton. “It’s inconceivable to me.”
 Regardless of the outcome, the felony charges have serious consequences for the accused. In the days since receiving the complaint, Sziszak has been forced to manage the expense of hiring an attorney and the frustration of navigating a legal system upended by the coronavirus pandemic. Even if the charges don’t result in a sentence, the filing could intimidate protesters who want to legally record police, or make others afraid to engage with them.
 “I cannot explain the fear I have and the worry I have for me vs. a police department,” Szisznak writes. “I feel afraid that I may have compromised my whole future based on something that I had believed was exercising my First Amendment right.”
title: 新泽西州警察局。对五名Twitter用户提出网络骚扰指控，原因是他们在抗议期间分享了一张照片，寻求一名蒙面警察的身份
title_en: A NJ police dept. filed cyber harassment charges against five Twitter users for sharing a photo seeking the identity of a masked police officer during protests

note:
Twitter的iOS应用程序周三更新称，该应用程序现在将允许所有用户限制谁可以回复他们的推文，但事实证明，该公司可能已经抢先一步。
Twitter自5月份以来一直在试验这一功能，并于昨日对其移动应用程序的iPhone版本进行了更新，暗示这一功能将被更广泛地推出。Twitter的一位发言人现在告诉The Verge，它不小心推错了发布说明；实际上，就目前而言，这一功能并没有更广泛地推出。
App Store上的更新文字写道：“在5月份，我们测试了一种与你想要的人聊天的新方式，这样你就可以创造和消费更有意义的改变。”“现在，每个人都可以尝试这一新功能，并选择谁可以回复他们的推文。”
Twitter的一位发言人说，这种误解是由于“一些不小心推送的发布说明”，“目前并不是每个人都可以使用限制推文回复的功能。”目前还不清楚这一功能在最新更新中是否有任何有意义的扩展，但Twitter似乎仍在测试回复限制，然后才能让更多用户使用这一功能。
如果你确实有访问权限，用户只需在写一条“每个人都可以回复”的推文时，只需点击键盘上方的方框就可以使用这一功能。然后，他们可以在三个选项中进行选择：每个人都可以回复他们的推文，只有他们关注的人才能回复，只有他们提到的人才能回复。选择一个选项并不会使其成为未来推文的默认选项。
这一功能是Twitter近几个月来更有趣的变化之一，它创造了新的表情包和在平台上交谈的方式。限制回复可能是避免骚扰的一个很好的方法，但它也可以用来限制对话，以一种感觉与Twitter的最基本功能背道而驰的方式。
更正：本文的前一个版本引用了Twitter的发布说明，声明其回复限制功能已为所有用户启用。Twitter在发布后通知我们，它的发布说明是不正确的。这篇报道已经更新，包括推特的更正。
note_en:
An update to Twitter’s iOS app on Wednesday said the app would now let all users to limit who can reply to their tweets, but it turns out the company may have jumped the gun.
 Twitter has been experimenting with this feature  since May, and yesterday pushed an update to the iPhone version of its mobile app suggesting the feature would be more widely rolling out. A Twitter spokesperson now tells  The Verge it accidentally pushed incorrect release notes; the feature is not in fact more widely rolling out, for now.
  “In May, we tested a new way to have a chat with exactly who you want, so you can create and consume more meaningful changes,” read the  update text on the App Store. “Now, everyone can try this new feature and choose who can reply to their tweets.”
 A Twitter spokesperson says the misconception was due “to some accidentally pushed release notes,” and that “the ability to limit replies on tweets is not currently available to everyone.” It’s not clear then whether the feature has been expanded in any meaningful fashion with the latest update, but Twitter appears to still be testing reply limiting before making the feature available to more users.
 If you do have access, users can use the feature by simply tapping the box above the keyboard when composing a tweet that says “Everyone can reply.” They can then choose between three options: everyone can reply to their tweet, only people they follow can reply, and only people they mention can reply. Choosing one option doesn’t make that the default for future tweets.
   The feature has been one of Twitter’s more interesting changes in recent months, creating  new memes and ways to talk on the platform. Reply limiting can be a great way to avoid harassment, but it can also be used to limit conversations in a way that feels counterintuitive to Twitter’s most basic functions.
 Correction: A previous version of this article cited release notes from Twitter stating that its reply-limiting feature had been enabled for all users. Twitter informed us after publication that its release notes were incorrect. This story has been updated to include Twitter’s correction.
title: Twitter表示，周三不小心推送了错误的iOS应用发布说明，称现在将允许所有用户限制谁可以回复他们的推文
title_en: Twitter says it accidentally pushed incorrect iOS app release notes on Wednesday that said it would now let all users limit who can reply to their tweets

note:
所有公共EEG数据集的列表。这份脑电图资源清单并不详尽。如果您发现了一些新的东西，或者已经深入研究了任何未过滤的链接，请更新存储库。
左/右手MI：包括52名受试者(38名具有区别性特征的有效受试者)、生理和心理问卷结果、肌电数据集、3D EEG电极位置和非任务相关状态的脑电图。
运动/图像数据集：包括109名志愿者、64个电极、2个基线任务(睁眼和闭眼)、运动运动和运动图像(双拳或双脚)。
抓举脑电图挑战赛：12名受试者，32个通道，500 Hz，共6个抓举项目，即a)。手动启动b)。FirstDigitTouch c)。开始加载阶段d)。发射e)。替换f)。两者都已发布。
运动图像的最大SCP数据：该数据集包含60小时的EEG BCI记录，涉及13名参与者的75个记录会话、60,000个心理图像和4个BCI交互范式，其中包含同一个体的多个记录会话和范式。脑机接口交互涉及多达6个心理意象状态被考虑。[文章]。
脑机接口竞赛IV-1：64个脑电通道，1000 Hz采样率，左手、右手、脚2类(+空闲状态)，7名受试者。评估数据是也包含空闲状态周期的连续EEG。
脑机接口竞赛IV-2a：22个电极的脑电图运动图像数据集，有9个受试者和2个会话，每个受试者有288个4秒的想象动作试验。包括左手、右手、脚和舌头的运动。[数据集描述]。
脑机接口竞赛IV-2b：3电极EEG运动图像数据集，有9个受试者和5个想象左手或右手运动的会话，最新的3个会话包括在线反馈。[数据集描述]
高伽马数据集：128个电极的数据集，来自14个健康受试者，每个受试者有大约1000个4秒的执行动作试验，分为13次。这四类动作分别是左手、右手、双脚和休息的动作。
左/右手1D/2D动作：1个受试者的19个电极数据，1D和2D手部动作的各种组合(实际执行)。
右手拇指动作想象：每次试验都要求受试者休息，记录休息数据5min。此外，当受试者被要求想象右手拇指运动时，也记录了5秒的历时数据。5个想象的运动，并记录每个试验的休息状态。单个受试者，8个电极，256 Hz。
心理表象数据集：13名受试者，超过6万例运动表象，采用38通道医用脑电图系统记录的4个交互范式中的6万多个动作表象。它包含多达6个主要用于运动的心理想象的数据。[文章]。
DAP：包括32个受试者，每个受试者观看1分钟长的音乐视频节选，由用户根据arousal/valence/like-dislike/dominanace/famaliarity，和22/32子视频的正面记录进行评级。
ENTERFACE&#39；06项目07：EEG(64个通道)+fNIRS+FACE视频，包括16个受试者，其中情绪是通过选择的IAPS数据集的子集引起的。
想象的情绪：31名受试者听暗示一种情绪的语音录音，并要求受试者想象一种情绪情景或回忆他们以前感受过这种情绪的经历。
神经营销：25名受试者，14个电极，喜欢/不喜欢的商业电子商务产品超过14类，每类3张图片。数据集的文章：脑电信号的分析及其在神经营销中的应用。[文章]
SEED：向15名受试者播放引发积极/消极/中性情绪的视频剪辑，并记录超过62个通道的脑电图。
SEED-IV：向15名受试者播放快乐/悲伤/中性/恐惧情绪的视频剪辑，并在62个通道上记录脑电图(带有眼球跟踪)，每名受试者3次(每次24次试验)。
SEED-VIG：在模拟驾驶任务中使用脑电数据进行警戒标记。包括18个电极和眼球跟踪。
HCI标记：让受试者观看视频片段(电影片段)，并要求他们在价位和唤醒程度上对情绪状态进行注释。在整个实验过程中，音频、视频、凝视数据和生理数据被同步记录，传感器之间精确同步。
唤醒调节：18名受试者接受三种不同的音频反馈沉默、假的和脑-机接口的在线飞行模拟器研究。[文章]。
BCI-NER挑战：26名受试者，56个脑电通道，用于P300拼写任务，并标记了当P300解码正确或错误字母时引发的反应的数据集。
在目标选择任务中监测ERRP：6名受试者有64个脑电电极，看着光标朝目标方块移动，并根据光标在正确或错误的方向上移动来标记诱发的反应。[数据集描述]。
连续反馈过程中的ERRP：10名受试者28个脑电电极，玩视频游戏研究执行和结果错误。[数据集部件-1][数据集部件-2]
HCI标记：向Subjetc显示图像或电影片段，并在屏幕底部添加标记。在某些情况下，标签正确地描述了一些情况。然而，在其他情况下，标签并不实际应用于媒体项目。在每个项目之后，如果参与者同意适用于媒体项目的标签，则要求他们按绿色按钮，如果不同意，则按红色按钮。在整个实验过程中，音频、视频、凝视数据和生理数据被同步记录，传感器之间精确同步。
C-VEP BCI：9名受试者，32个EEG通道，用于VEP BCI拼写任务(32个字符)，以及针对与拼写相关联的标签所引起的响应的标记数据集。[数据集说明][已发表文章]。
带干电极的C-VEP BCI：9名受试者，15个干EEG通道，用于VEP BCI拼写任务(32个字符)，以及针对与拼写相关联的标签所引起的响应的标记数据集。[文章]。
SSVEP-视觉搜索/辨别和握手：包括3种不同的测试，(I)五盒视觉测试：基于附着力和无人值守的圆盘和正方形刺激，(Ii)自然图像内的视觉搜索：在黑白自然图像中搜索黄点刺激，(Iii)握手测试：显示左手/右手闭合/打开图像。30名受试者，14个电极。[第1条][第2条][更多数据集：数据集2]。
同步脑波数据集：15人被呈现两种不同的视频刺激，包括眨眼、放松、心算、数颜色框和观看超级碗广告。[刺激计划1][刺激计划2]。
图形视觉诱发电位：数据集#5，2名受试者在O1位置记录棋盘光图形(古怪范例)。
面孔VS房屋歧视：7名癫痫患者分别接受了50个灰度刺激，分别用于脸部和房屋图片。对于每个受试者，总共进行了3次实验，产生了300次刺激。
目标与非目标：25名受试者测试大脑入侵者，这是一个使用古怪范式的视觉P300脑机接口。16个电极，湿的。出版物，代码。数据集ID：BI.EEG.2012-GIPSA。
目标与非目标：24名受试者玩大脑入侵者，这是一个使用古怪范式的视觉P300脑机接口。16个电极，湿的。每名受试者最多参加8次会议。两种实验条件：使用黎曼几何进行自适应校准和不使用自适应校准。出版物，代码。数据集ID：BI.EEG.2013-GIPSA。
目标与非目标：71名受试者玩大脑入侵者，这是一种视觉P300脑机接口，使用具有自适应黎曼几何的古怪范式(无校准)。16个电极，干燥。出版物，代码。数据集ID：bi2014a.。
目标与非目标：38名受试者玩多人协作版本的大脑入侵者，这是一个使用具有自适应黎曼几何(无校准)的古怪范式的视觉P300脑机接口。每名受试者32个电极，湿的，每次治疗2名受试者。出版物，代码。数据集ID：bi2014b.。
目标与非目标：50名受试者玩大脑入侵者，这是一种视觉P300脑机接口，使用具有自适应黎曼几何的古怪范式(无校准)。32个电极，湿的。每名受试者3次，并调节闪光持续时间。出版物，代码。数据集ID：bi2015a.。
目标对非目标：44名受试者玩多人(合作和竞争)版本的大脑入侵者，这是一个使用具有自适应黎曼几何(无校准)的古怪范式的视觉P300脑机接口。每名受试者32个电极，湿式，每次2名受试者。出版物，代码。数据集ID：bi2015b.。
阻抗数据：12名受试者接受20%稀有刺激的P300任务(Oddball范式)。总共有128个目标刺激和512个标准刺激。以一种方式收集数据集，使得一次记录包含电极中的不同阻抗。[文章]。
持续注意驾驶：27名被试在VR环境下进行持续注意驾驶，以监测事件相关电位。每个受试者都参加了两次90min的训练(无/无和有动觉反馈)，并用32个通道和500 Hz进行记录。[文章][预处理数据集]。
德莱亚德-言语：5个不同的实验，通过包括听觉、视觉刺激和想象言语在内的各种任务来研究自然言语理解。(I)20世纪中叶一部受欢迎的美国小说的有声读物版本--19名受试者，(Ii)以相同的顺序介绍相同的审判，但28个演讲片段中的每一个都是反向播放的，(Iii)N400实验：受试者阅读300个句子，其中一半以不一致的词结束--(Iv)鸡尾酒会实验：33个受试者进行了30个实验，每个实验60分，其中两个经典小说作品被呈现给他们：一个放在左耳，另一个放在左耳。受试者被分成两组，每组17人和16人(排除对象+1人)，在整个30个试验中，每组人都被指示用左耳或右耳听故事。(V)多感官实验：刺激是从一组视频中提取的，这些视频由一名男性以类似对话的方式说美国英语。[正文][补充条款]
心理表象数据集：13名受试者，超过6万例运动表象，采用38通道医用脑电图系统记录的4个交互范式中的6万多个动作表象。它包含多达6个主要用于运动的心理想象的数据。[文章]。
静息状态脑电图数据：22名受试者，72个脑电通道，进行8min的静息任务，闭眼4min，睁眼4min。[文章]。
EID-M、EID-S：8例静息状态(闭眼)受试者，用EPOC+记录14个电极，记录时间为54s，频率为128 Hz(每电极7000个样本)。EID-M有三个试验，EID-S是一个单一试验数据集。数据集被用来开发一个通过脑电波进行身份识别的系统。[文章]。
SPIS静息状态数据集：10名受试者，64个通道，每种状态(闭眼和睁眼)记录2.5分钟，然后用固定顺序和变化的ISIS持续关注反应任务105分钟。[艺术]。
α波-20名受试者，16个通道，两个触发器(A)闭眼和(B)睁眼的10s样本。标签也可用于疲劳(从1到10)。
音乐图像信息检索：10名受试者，64个脑电通道，完成12个不同节拍、长度和节奏的音乐图像任务。[文章]。
面部感知过程中的非自愿眼动：数据集1，26个电极，500 Hz采样率，120次试验。眼球运动和瞳孔直径记录、EEG和EOG数据是当受试者在屏幕上呈现出高兴/悲伤/愤怒的面孔时出现的。[文章][附注：数据集仅按要求提供]。
自愿-非自愿眨眼：使用g.tec在14个电极上记录20名受试者的自愿眨眼(要求受试者在音频刺激后1s内自愿眨眼)和非自愿眨眼(自然眨眼)。对于每个主题，以.mat格式提供3个会议，每个会议有20个试验。[文章]
EEG-EY STATE：带有闭眼和睁眼标签的一次连续记录117秒的EEG的眼睛状态标签数据。该数据集是从Emotiv耳机记录的。
EEG-IO：使用OpenBCI设备和Biopac Cap100C记录20例受试者的自愿性单眼眨眼(给予外部刺激)和额部电极(FP1、FP2)的脑电图。进行了一次会议，每个受试者大约25次眨眼。手动注释是使用视频馈送完成的。[文章]。
EEG-VV、EEG-VR：使用OpenBCI设备和Biopac Cap100C记录12例受试者的非自主眨眼(自然眨眼)和额部电极(FP1、FP2)的脑电图。受试者进行了两项活动--观看视频(EEG-VV)和阅读文章(EEG-VR)。手动注释是使用视频馈送完成的。[文章]。
眼睛状态预测：通过使用EPOC耳机(14个电极)记录标记的眼睛状态数据(打开和关闭)，117秒记录单个受试者。[文章]。
KARA-ONE：想象和发声的音素和单字提示，以获取语言和语音产品。14名受试者使用64通道神经扫描Quick-cap进行记录，同时进行面部跟踪和音频。[文章]。
MNIST大脑数字：当一个数字(0-9)被显示给受试者时的脑电图数据，使用Minwave、EPOC、Muse、Insight为单个受试者记录2秒。包括超过120万个样品。
ImageNet Brain：显示随机图像(来自Imagenet ILSVRC2013列车数据集中的14k个图像)，并为一个受试者记录3s的EEG信号。包括超过70K个样本。
工作记忆：参与者短暂观察一个包含多个英文字符集(500ms)的数组，并将信息保持3秒。然后呈现测试字符，如果测试宪章与集合中的一个字符匹配，则参与者通过按下按钮来响应。15名学生，64个电极，500 Hz采样率。只有一小部分数据是公开的。[原文][ICLR的进一步分析]
深度睡眠慢振荡：在慢振荡结束前10秒开始录制的10秒。记录数据的目的是预测在假条件下，即没有任何刺激的情况下，慢振荡之后是否会有另一慢振荡。
酒精中毒的遗传易感性：对120名受试者进行120次试验，记录在256 Hz的64个电信号上。考虑了两组受试者，酗酒者和对照组。文中给出了具体的刺激方案。
MOOC期间的困惑：10名学生观看MOOC视频，分为两类-令人困惑的(如基础数学)和不令人困惑的(如量子理论)。时长2分钟，每个类别10个视频。通过正面通道的单通道无线心态录制。[文章]。
TUH EEG资源：(I)异常EEG和(Ii)EEG发作的大量数据
note_en:
A list of all public EEG-datasets. This list of EEG-resources is not exhaustive. If you find something new, or have explored any unfiltered link in depth, please update the repository.
 Left/Right Hand MI: Includes 52 subjects (38 validated subjects with discriminative features), results of physiological and psychological questionnares, EMG Datasets, location of 3D EEG electrodes, and EEGs for non-task related states
 Motor Movement/Imagery Dataset: Includes 109 volunteers, 64 electrodes, 2 baseline tasks (eye-open and eye-closed), motor movement, and motor imagery (both fists or both feet)
 Grasp and Lift EEG Challenge: 12 subjects, 32channels@500Hz, for 6 grasp and lift events, namely a). HandStart b). FirstDigitTouch c). BothStartLoadPhase d). LiftOff e). Replace f). BothReleased
 The largest SCP data of Motor-Imagery: The dataset contains 60 hours of EEG BCI recordings across 75 recording sessions of 13 participants, 60,000 mental imageries, and 4 BCI interaction paradigms, with multiple recording sessions and paradigms of the same individuals. BCI interactions involving up to 6 mental imagery states are considered.  [Article]
 BCI Competition IV-1: 64 EEG channels at 1000Hz sampling rate for 2 classes of left hand, right hand, foot (+ idle state) for 7 subjects. Evaluation data is continuous EEG which contains also periods of idle state.
 BCI Competition IV-2a: 22-electrode EEG motor-imagery dataset, with 9 subjects and 2 sessions, each with 288 four-second trials of imagined movements per subject. Includes movements of the left hand,the right hand, the feet and the tongue.  [Dataset Description]
 BCI Competition IV-2b: 3-electrode EEG motor-imagery dataset with 9 subjects and 5 sessions of imagined movements of the left or the right hand, the latest 3 sessions include online feedback.  [Dataset Description]
 High-Gamma Dataset: 128-electrode dataset obtained from 14 healthy subjects with roughly 1000 four-second trials of executed movements divided into 13 runs per subject. The four classes of movements were movements of either the left hand, the right hand, both feet, and rest.
 Left/Right Hand 1D/2D movements: 19-electrode data of one subject with various combinations of 1D and 2D hand movements (actual execution).
 Imagination of Right-hand Thumb Movement: In every trial, subjects were asked to rest and rest data was recorded for 5 mins. Further, 5 second epoch data was also recorded when subjects were asked to imagine right hand thumb movement. 5 of such imagined motor movement, and rest state was recorded for each trial. Single subject, 8 electrodes at 256Hz.
 Mental-Imagery Dataset: 13 participants with over 60,000 examples of motor imageries in 4 interaction paradigms recorded with 38 channels medical-grade EEG system. It contains data for upto 6 mental imageries primarily for the motor moements.  [Article]
 DEAP: Includes 32 subjects, each watchine 1-min long excerpts of music-videos, rated by users in terms of arousal/valence/like-dislike/dominanace/famaliarity, and frontal face recording of 22/32 subejcts.
 Enterface'06: Enterface'06 Project 07: EEG(64 Channels) + fNIRS + face video, Includes 16 subjects, where emotions were elicited through selected subset of IAPS dataset.
 Imagined Emotion: 31 subjects, subjects listen to voice recordings that suggest an emotional feeling and ask subjects to imagine an emotional scenario or to recall an experience in which they have felt that emotion before.
 NeuroMarketing: 25 subjects, 14 electrodes, Like/Dislike on commercial e-commerce products over 14 categories with 3 images each. Article for the dataset: Analysis of EEG signals and its application to neuromarketing.  [Article]
 SEED: 15 subjects were shown video clips eliciting positive/negative/neutral emotion and EEG was recorded over 62 channels.
 SEED-IV: 15 subjects were shown video clips ellicity happy/sad/neutral/fear emotions and EEG was recorded over 62 channels (with eye-tracking) for 3 sessions per subject (24 trials per session).
 SEED-VIG: Vigilance labels with EEG data in a simulated driving task. 18 electrodes and eye-tracking included.
 HCI-Tagging: Subjetcs were shown video clips (fragments of movies) and they were asked to annotate the emotional state on the scale of valence and arousal. During the whole experiment, audio, video, gaze data and physiological data were recorded simultaneously with accurate synchronisation between sensors.
 Regulation of Arousal: 18 subjects going through an online flight simulator study with three different audio-feedback silence, sham and BCI.  [Article]
 BCI-NER Challenge: 26 subjects, 56 EEG Channels for a P300 Speller task, and labeled dataset for the response elicited when P300 decodes a correct or incorrect letter.
 Monitoring ErrP in a target selection task: 6 subjects with 64 EEG electrodes, watching a cursor move towards a target square, and elicited responses are labeled based on whether the cursor moves in right or wrong direction.  [Dataset Description]
 ErrPs during continuous feedback: 10 subjects with 28 EEG electrodes, playing a video game to study execution and outcome error.  [Dataset Part-1]  [Dataset Part-2]
 HCI-Tagging: Subjetcs were shown images or movie fragments with a tag at the bottom of the screen. In some cases, the tag correctly described something about the situation. However, in other cases the tag did not actually apply to the media item. After each item, a participant was asked to press a green button if they agreed with the tag being applicable to the media item, or press a red button if not. During the whole experiment, audio, video, gaze data and physiological data were recorded simultaneously with accurate synchronisation between sensors.
 c-VEP BCI: 9 subjects, 32 EEG Channels for a VEP BCI speller (32 characters) task, and labeled dataset for the response elicited for the label associated with the speller.  [Dataset description]  [Published article]
 c-VEP BCI with dry electrodes: 9 subjects, 15 dry-EEG Channels for a VEP BCI speller (32 characters) task, and labeled dataset for the response elicited for the label associated with the speller.  [Article]
 SSVEP - Visual Search/Discrimination and Handshake: Includes 3 different tests, (i) Five Box visual test: attnded and unattended disc and square based stimuli, (ii) visual search within natural images: search of a yellow dot stimuli in B&W natural images, (iii) hand shake test: showing left/right hand closed/open images. 30 subjects, 14 electrodes.  [Article 1]  [Article 2]  [More Dataset: Dataset 2]
 Synchronized Brainwave Dataset: 15 people were presented with 2 different video stimulus including blinks, relaxation, mental mathematics, counting color boxes, and watching superbowl ads.  [Stimulus 1]  [Stimulus 2]
 Pattern Visual Evoked Potentials: Dataset#5, 2 subjects for checkboard light pattern (oddball paradigm) recorded at O1 position.
 Face vs. House Discrimination: 7 Epileptic subjects were presented with 50 grayscale stimulations each for Face and House pictures. For each subject, total 3 experimental runs were conducted resulting in 300 stimulations.
 Target Versus Non-Target: 25 subjects testing Brain Invaders, a visual P300 Brain-Computer Interface using oddball paradigm. 16-electrodes, wet.  publication,  code. Dataset id: BI.EEG.2012-GIPSA.
 Target Versus Non-Target: 24 subjects playing Brain Invaders, a visual P300 Brain-Computer Interface using oddball paradigm. 16-electrodes, wet. Up to 8 sessions per subject. Two experiemental conditions: with and without adaptive calibration using Riemannian geometry.  publication,  code. Dataset id: BI.EEG.2013-GIPSA.
 Target Versus Non-Target: 71 subjects playing Brain Invaders, a visual P300 Brain-Computer Interface using oddball paradigm with adapative Riemannian Geometry (no-calibration). 16-electrodes, dry.  publication,  code. Dataset id: bi2014a.
 Target Versus Non-Target: 38 subjects playing a multiplayer and collaborative version of Brain Invaders, a visual P300 Brain-Computer Interface using oddball paradigm with adapative Riemannian Geometry (no-calibration). 32-electrodes per subject, wet, 2 subjects during each session.  publication,  code. Dataset id: bi2014b.
 Target Versus Non-Target: 50 subjects playing Brain Invaders, a visual P300 Brain-Computer Interface using oddball paradigm with adapative Riemannian Geometry (no-calibration). 32-electrodes, wet. 3 sessions per subjects with modulation of flash duration.  publication,  code. Dataset id: bi2015a.
 Target Versus Non-Target: 44 subjects playing a multiplayer (cooperation and competition) version of Brain Invaders, a visual P300 Brain-Computer Interface using oddball paradigm with adapative Riemannian Geometry (no-calibration). 32-electrodes per subject, wet, 2 subjects for each session.  publication,  code. Dataset id: bi2015b.
 Impedance Data: 12 subjects for P300 task (Oddball paradigm) with 20% of rare stimuli. In total, there were 128 target stimuli and 512 standard stimuli. The dataset was collected in a way such that one recording contains different impedances in electrodes.  [Article]
 Sustained-Attention Driving: 27 subjects for sustained-attention driving in a VR settin for monitoring event-related potentials. Each subject participated in two 90 min sessions (w/o and with kinesthetic feedback) and recorded with 32-channels and 500Hz.  [Article]  [Pre-processed dataset]
 Dryad-Speech: 5 different experiments for studying natural speech comprehension through a variety of tasks including audio, visual stimulus and imagined speech. (i) Audio-book version of a popular mid-20th century American work of fiction - 19 subjects, (ii) presentation of the same trials in the same order, but with each of the 28 speech segments played in reverse, (iii) N400 experiment: subjects read 300 sentences presented with the rest of the sentence and half which ended with an incongruent word - , (iv) cocktail party experiment: 33 subjects undertook 30 trials, each of 60 s in length, where they were presented with 2 classic works of fiction: one to the left ear, and the other to the right ear. Subjects were divided into 2 groups of 17 and 16 (+1 excluded subject) with each group instructed to attend to the story in either the left or right ear throughout the entire 30 trials, (v) multisensory experiment: stimuli were drawn from a set of videos that consisted of a male speaking American English in a conversational-like manner.  [Main Article]  [Supplemntary Article]
 Mental-Imagery Dataset: 13 participants with over 60,000 examples of motor imageries in 4 interaction paradigms recorded with 38 channels medical-grade EEG system. It contains data for upto 6 mental imageries primarily for the motor moements.  [Article]
 Resting State EEG Data: 22 subjects, 72 EEG Channels for a resting task of 8 mins with 4 mins of eyes closed and 4 mins of eyes open.  [Article]
 EID-M, EID-S: 8 subjects in rest state (with eyes closed) recorded from 14 electrodes using EPOC+ for 54s at 128 Hz (7000 samples each). EID-M has three trials and EID-S is a signle trial dataset. The dataset was used to develop a person identification system through brainwaves.  [Article]
 SPIS Resting State Dataset: 10 subjects, 64 channels, 2.5 minutes recording in each state (eyes-closed and eyes-open) prior to a 105-minute session of Sustained Attention to Response Task with fixed-sequence and varying ISIs.  [Artcile]
 Alpha-waves 20 subjects, 16 channels, 10s samples of two triggers (a) eyes-closed, and (b) eyes-open. The labels are alaso availabe for fatigue (from 1-10).
 Music Imagery Information Retrieval: 10 subjects, 64 EEG Channels for a music imagery task of 12 different pieces w/ different meter, length and tempo.  [Article]
 Involuntary Eye Movements during Face Perception: Dataset 1, 26 electrodes, 500Hz sampling rate, and 120 trials. Eye movements and pupil diameter record, EEG and EOG data is present when subject is presented a happy/sad/angry face on the screen.  [Article] [P.S: Dataset available on request only]
 Voluntary-Involuntary Eye-Blinks: Voluntary eye-blinks (subject were asked to blink voluntarily within 1s of audio stimulus) and involuntary eye-blinks (natural) was recorded for 20 subjects on 14 electrodes using g.tec. For each subject, 3 sessions with 20 trials each are present in .mat format.  [Article]
 EEG-eye state: Eye-state labeled data for one continuous recording of EEG of 117 seconds with eye-closed and eye-open labels. The dataset was recorded from Emotiv headset.
 EEG-IO: Voluntary single eye-blinks (external stimulation was provided) and EEG was recorded for frontal electrodes (Fp1, Fp2) for 20 subjects using OpenBCI Device and BIOPAC Cap100C. One session was conducted including around 25 blinks per subject. Manual annotation was done using video feed.  [Article]
 EEG-VV, EEG-VR: Involuntary eye-blinks (natural blinks) and EEG was recorded for frontal electrodes (Fp1, Fp2) for 12 subjects using OpenBCI Device and BIOPAC Cap100C. Subjects performed two activities - watching a video (EEG-VV) and reading an article (EEG-VR). Manual annotation was done using video feed.  [Article]
 Eye State Prediction: 117 seconds recording of a single subject with labeled eye state data (open and closed) recorded using EPOC headset (14 electrodes).  [Article]
 Kara-One: Imagined and vocalized phonemic and single-word prompts to access the language and speech production. 14 subjects recorded using 64-channel Neuroscan Quick-cap, along with face tracking and audio.  [Article]
 MNIST Brain Digits: EEG data when a digit(0-9) is shown to the subject, recorded 2s for a single subject using Minwave, EPOC, Muse, Insight. Includes over 1.2M samples.
 Imagenet Brain: A random image is shown (out of 14k images from the Imagenet ILSVRC2013 train dataset) and EEG signals are recorded for 3s for one subject. Includes over 70k samples.
 Working Memory: Participants briefly observe an array containing multiple English characters SET (500ms) and maintain the information for three seconds. A TEST character is then presented and participants respond by press of a button if TEST charter matches one of the characters in the SET. 15 students, 64 electrodes and 500Hz sampling rate. Only a small subset of data is available publicly.  [Original Paper]  [Further Analysis in ICLR]
 Deep Sleep Slow Osciallation: 10 seconds of recording starting 10 seconds before the end of a slow oscillation. Data is recorded with a goal to predict whether or not a slow oscillation will be followed by another one in sham condition, i.e. without any stimulation.
 Genetic Predisposition to Alcoholism: 120 trials for 120 subjects recorded from 64 electrides at 256Hz. Two groups of subjects were considered, alcoholic and control. Stimuli details are given in the paper.
 Confusion during MOOC: 10 students watching MOOC videos in two categories - confusing (e.g., basic maths) and non-confusing (e.g., quantum theory). 2-minute duration 10 videos in each category. Recorded from single-channel wireless MindSet over frontal channel.  [Article]
 TUH EEG Resources: Massive amount of data for (i) Abnormal EEG and (ii) EEG Seizures

一名戴着防护面罩的行人走过一家被木板封住的J.Crew Group Inc.。美国华盛顿特区第14街的商店，2020年4月16日(星期四)。周三，市长穆里尔·鲍瑟(Muriel Bowser)宣布将非必要业务的关闭延长至原定4月24日之后的5月15日。，彭博社。
彭博社(Bloomberg)--随着大流行加剧了美国零售商的困境，J.Crew集团(J.Crew Group Inc.)的公司。对于安·泰勒的所有者来说，他们正在利用破产法第11章的破产申请，迅速摆脱昂贵的长期租约，关闭数千家门店。
通过寻求法院保护，像内曼·马库斯集团(Neiman Marcus Group Inc.)这样的公司。而男装的母公司避免了与个别房东进行旷日持久的谈判的头疼问题。但这些举措可能会颠覆大片房地产市场和5000亿美元的商业抵押贷款支持证券市场。
前破产法官、现在是奥特堡律师事务所(Otterburg PC)合伙人的梅勒妮·西加诺夫斯基(Melanie Cyganowski)表示：“这现在是黑字法--债务人可以把房东塞进去。”“如果这变成一场零售商拒绝租约的海啸，将会引发另一部分沧桑巨变--房东持有的抵押贷款。”
像J.C.Penney Co.和Brooks Brothers Group Inc.这样破产的公司。看看丢弃租约，房东已经感受到了后果。CBL&Amp；Associates Properties Inc.在美国拥有100多个购物中心，在租金收入陷入困境后，该公司正在准备自己的破产申请。根据研究公司Trepp的数据，7月份捆绑到CMBS中的零售房地产贷款中有16%拖欠。
彭博社(Bloomberg)汇编的数据显示，今年至少有25家主要零售商申请破产。最新加入的公司包括男装和乔斯的所有者Tailed Brands Inc.。答：银行(Bank)正寻求关闭其1200多家门店中的约三分之一，洛德·安培·泰勒(Lord&Amp；Taylor)的母公司Le Tote表示，可能会关闭百货商店剩余的所有门店。
为房东工作的罗宾逊·布罗格·莱因旺律师事务所(Robinson Brog Leinwand Greene Genovese&amp；Gluck P.C.Ringel)负责商业融资和重组业务的联席主席弗雷德·林格尔(Fred Ringel)说，“这既经济又高效，而且能让零售商迅速理顺业务。”他说，他现在比以往任何时候都更忙于重新谈判租约，在某些情况下，他还会说服租户放弃取消订单，按修改后的条款入住。
以维生素零售商GNC控股公司为例。它在全国经营着数百家门店，大部分在露天购物中心。法庭记录显示，自6月份申请破产以来，GNC已要求拒绝至少500份租约，以及50多份特许经营协议和转租。
与此同时，查克·E·芝士(Chuck E.Cheese)的母公司CEC娱乐公司(CEC Entertainment Inc.)在6月份申请破产后，正在与房东进行谈判。该公司本周获得法院批准，在评估希望保留哪些地点时推迟支付租金。
西班牙零售商Desigual的美国子公司表示，在努力从房东那里获得租金减免后，该公司被迫提交申请。该公司的一名代表在法庭文件中表示：“不幸的是，杜莎在让房东认识到新的现实方面收效甚微，即大多数租户--特别是零售业租户--无力支付新冠肺炎之前的租金，”该公司的一名代表在法庭文件中表示。
反过来，房东也有自己的抵押贷款要担心，这些抵押贷款也是以大流行前关于租金收取的假设为担保的。在这种压力下，巴里·斯特恩利赫特(Barry Sternlicht)的喜达屋资本集团(Starwood Capital Group)未能偿还与五个购物中心相关的证券化债务，萨克斯的所有者哈德逊湾公司(Hudson‘s Bay Co.)也跳过了某些CMBS的到期利息。根据Trepp的数据，捆绑在债券中的零售抵押贷款违约率从1月份的3.8%攀升至7月份的16%。
一些零售商可以在不申请破产的情况下制定租金减免和其他租赁修改，包括终止。然而，希尔科房地产公司(Hilco Real Estate)执行副总裁纳文·纳格拉尼(Navin Nagrani)表示，在法庭程序之外谈判数百笔交易可能是具有挑战性的，特别是对于可能有数百名房东要打交道的大型零售连锁店来说。
破产将权力从房东转到租户手中。零售商可以合法地在法庭上拒绝一批租约，有时让建筑物所有者只收取几分钱。公司还可以将有利的合同出售给其他方，以帮助偿还债权人。
“有时破产是摆脱这些租约的最有利方式，”Nagrani说。
根据CoreSight Research的数据，预计到2020年，美国将有多达2.5万家门店关闭，其中大部分位于购物中心。百货商店和时尚精品店被视为最濒危的。
根据房地产研究公司Green Street Advisors 4月份的一份报告，到2021年底，超过一半的购物中心百货商店可能会永久关闭。J.C.Penney上个月表示，将关闭150多家门店，而Neiman Marcus计划退出纽约哈德逊庭院(Hudson Yards)的开发项目，并关闭其他三家美国门店。
高纬物业(Cushman&amp；Wakefield)美国零售业研究主管加里克·布朗(Garrick Brown)表示，迄今为止的关闭只是“冰山一角”。他说，在接下来的两年里，至少有12亿平方英尺(占已经入驻的商店地产的10%)将空置。“最坏的情况是，这个数字可能会翻一番。”
note_en:
A pedestrian wearing a protective mask walks past a boarded up J. Crew Group Inc. store on 14th Street in Washington, D.C., U.S., on Thursday, April 16, 2020. On Wednesday Mayor Muriel Bowser announced an extension of the shutdown of nonessential businesses until May 15 after the original date of April 24. , Bloomberg
   (Bloomberg) -- With the pandemic intensifying the plight of U.S. retailers, companies from J. Crew Group Inc. to the owner of Ann Taylor are using Chapter 11 bankruptcy filings to quickly get out of costly, long-term leases and shutter thousands of stores.
 By seeking court protection, firms like Neiman Marcus Group Inc. and the parent company of Men’s Wearhouse avoid the headache of protracted negotiations with individual landlords. But the moves threaten to upend huge swaths of the real estate market and the half-trillion dollar market for commercial mortgage-backed securities.
 “This is now black-letter law -- a debtor can cram down a landlord,” said Melanie Cyganowski, a former bankruptcy judge who’s now a partner at law firm Otterbourg PC. “If this becomes a tsunami of retailers rejecting their leases, it’s going to trigger another part of the sea change -- the mortgages held by the landlords.”
 As bankrupt firms like J.C. Penney Co. and Brooks Brothers Group Inc. look to jettison leases, landlords are already feeling the consequences. CBL & Associates Properties Inc., owner of more than 100 shopping centers in the U.S., is preparing its own bankruptcy filing after rent collections cratered. And 16% of retail property loans bundled into CMBS were delinquent in July, according to research firm Trepp.
  At least 25 major retailers have filed for bankruptcy this year, according to data compiled by Bloomberg. The most recent additions include Tailored Brands Inc., owner of Men’s Wearhouse and Jos. A. Bank, which is seeking to close about a third of its more than 1,200 stores, and Lord & Taylor parent company Le Tote, which said it could shut down all of the department store’s remaining locations.
 “It’s economical, it’s efficient and it allows retailers to rationalize their footprint quickly,” said Fred Ringel, co-chair of the business finance and restructuring practice at the law firm Robinson Brog Leinwand Greene Genovese & Gluck P.C. Ringel, who works for landlords, said he’s busier than ever renegotiating leases and in some cases persuading tenants to forgo cancellations and stay under modified terms.
 Take vitamin retailer GNC Holdings Inc. It operates hundreds of stores across the country, mostly in strip malls. Since filing for bankruptcy in June, GNC has asked to reject at least 500 leases, along with more than 50 franchise agreements and subleases, according to court records.
  Meanwhile, CEC Entertainment Inc., the parent company of Chuck E. Cheese, is negotiating with its landlords after its June bankruptcy filing. It won court approval this week to defer rent payments as it evaluates which locations it wants to keep open.
 And the U.S. unit of Spanish retailer Desigual said it was forced to file after struggling to get rent abatements from its landlords. “Unfortunately, DUSA had little success in getting landlords to realize the new reality that most tenants -- especially those in retail -- cannot afford to pay pre-Covid-19 rent,” a representative for the firm said in court papers.
 Landlords, in turn, have their own mortgages to worry about, which were also underwritten with pre-pandemic assumptions about rent collections. Amid the stress, Barry Sternlicht’s Starwood Capital Group missed payments on securitized debt linked to five shopping malls, and Saks owner Hudson’s Bay Co. also skipped interest due on certain CMBS. Delinquencies on retail mortgages bundled into bonds climbed to 16% in July, from 3.8% in January, according to Trepp.
  Some retailers can work out rent abatements and other lease modifications including terminations without filing for bankruptcy. However, negotiating hundreds of deals outside of a court process can be challenging, especially for big retail chains that may have hundreds of landlords to deal with, said Navin Nagrani, an executive vice president at Hilco Real Estate.
 Bankruptcy flips the power from landlords to tenants. Retailers can legally reject a swath of leases in court, sometimes leaving building owners to collect just pennies on the dollar. Firms can also sell off favorable contracts to other parties to help repay creditors.
 “Sometimes a bankruptcy is the most advantageous way to get out of those leases,” Nagrani said.
 As many as 25,000 stores are expected to close in the U.S. in 2020, mostly in shopping malls, according to Coresight Research. Department stores and fashion boutiques are seen as the most endangered.
 More than half of mall department stores could close for good by the end of 2021, according to an April report from real estate research firm Green Street Advisors. J.C. Penney said last month that it would shutter more than 150 locations, while Neiman Marcus plans to pull out of New York’s Hudson Yards development and close three other U.S. locations.
 The closures so far are “just the tip of the iceberg,” said Garrick Brown, head of Americas retail research for Cushman & Wakefield. Over the next two years, at least 1.2 billion of square feet -- 10% of already-occupied store real estate -- will go vacant, he said. “Worst-case scenario, that could double.”

note:
这是你专注于加密货币的博客的背景图片，也是你的Twitter简历的背景图片，我们选择它作为代表Taaalk的图片。
这张图片对你意味着什么，它与你对比特币的看法有什么关系？
JMG不是比特币的人，如果说有什么不同的话，那就是更像是一个黄金狂热分子。但他的观点，即我们已经在技术上达到顶峰，未来看起来更像过去，而不是一些星际迷航乌托邦或银翼杀手反乌托邦，这一观点引起了我的共鸣。对我来说，比特币最代表的是向金本位制的回归。只是它将是比特币的标准。因为比特币更便于携带和交易。
正如米尔莎·波佩斯库(Mircea Popescu)在“比特币与穷人”一书中所说，比特币即使不是彻底的耶稣，也是自维多利亚女王(Queen Victoria)以来最保守的东西。比特币使得所谓的累进税收计划行不通。比特币使任何形式的公益都站不住脚。比特币让任何人假装与其他任何人平等的说法变得可笑。比特币不是用来让公社运作的，但恰恰相反：它会让公社变得无法运作，几乎每个人都不会感兴趣。
我不是像波佩斯库那样黑暗的启蒙运动巨魔，但有很多地方我是同意的。比特币不是坐在篝火旁的未来主义的金字塔。这是对自然、对极限、对传统价值观的回归，在很大程度上，它可能会让人感觉像是回到了更早的时代。
我认为，在超比特化之后，一些国家可能会保留一些现代福利国家的痕迹。这不会是件容易的事。不会有全民基本收入(UBI)。抱歉的。
首先，似乎比特币对上述未来的发展是有帮助的。这对我来说并不明显。这样对吗？如果是这样的话，为什么会是这样呢？
在金本位制下，政府对你的钱所能做的是有限度的。假设统治阶级想要发动一场战争，或者为你当地的社区大学建造一个新的体育场。他们可以征税。或者他们可以借钱。如果他们借钱，他们必须用黄金偿还借来的钱，就像“权力的游戏”中的铁银行一样。
如果黄金真的是这样运作的，那么金本位制实际上会设定一些限制。
实际上，谁需要黄金标准--我们可以像上帝希望的那样，什么都用黄金交易。不需要政府货币。
我们有政府货币，因为黄金实际上并不像我们柏拉图式的理想。
1、黄金不适用于小额交易。即使711酒类商店想要接受黄金，你也不能用它买一杯冰沙。你不能在金子里交易。因为它会被吹走然后迷路。
2、黄金出奇地容易被伪造。易趣上有很多涂着金色的钨条。这个足够好，可以通过水量测试。你们有X射线结晶仪吗？
比特币胜出的房产还有很多。但我认为，即使没有3，如果我们只有1和2，我们可能会有一个无处不在的黄金交易世界，就像奇幻小说中的那样。
但由于黄金很容易伪造，我们需要政府在硬币上盖章，由政府警察来惩罚造假者。你得付钱给警察。它很贵。
由于黄金不适用于小额交易，因此我们有小面额硬币和纸币。
让我们加4吧，也许是最重要的一个。黄金很难拿到。所以我们把它放在我们最信任的地方。政府银行。我们用的是可以兑换黄金的纸。当统治阶级想要战争时，他们在政府中的走狗太诱人了，他们会说，对不起，你的比索过去值1/16盎司黄金，现在值1/23盎司。不过，别担心。经济需要它。尼克松。罗斯福。德国。英国。罗马帝国。每个人都这么做了。所以这不是说人们在它发生了这么多次之后没有预见到它的到来。但黄金实在太难保护了，真的没有更好的选择了。
你可以争辩说，玩弄战争的奴才只是一个稻草人。想要促进社会平等的善意的民选领导人就更糟了！每个人都想消费，消费，不管你是否同意他们的理想。
回到第四点，安全存储。在“赛拉斯·马纳”中，这位织工一生都在努力积攒黄金，并把它藏在他的小茅屋的地板上。然后，这个放荡的上流社会的家伙找出了他把钱藏在哪里，就把它拿去还了一笔赌债。
使用比特币就不那么容易了！其实也没那么难。如果邓斯坦拿到了塞拉斯的硬件钱包纸备份，而塞拉斯没有使用密码短语，他可能会得到所有的密码。但是塞拉斯可以使用密码短语。Silas可以使用2取2或3取2的Multisig进行深度冷藏，并将其他密钥保存在他信任的人手中。或者更现实地说，因为他有信任问题，至少在比他的小屋地板更难接近的地方。
人们说，比特币太难使用了，没有人能费这么大劲来保护一个硬件钱包。好的，让你的银行保管好它。关键是，比特币给了你一个选择。
黄金的问题是，由于它的物理属性，你别无选择，只能信任第三方金库。一旦你这样做了，最好的选择就是信任政府的央行金库。而且不能相信政府不会贬值。
比特币的工作方式与黄金爱好者想象的黄金工作方式一样。在我看来，这就是为什么你需要比特币来获得真正的金本位。或者更确切地说，是比特币标准。
PS：保护硬件钱包的一个很好的方法是把它嵌入一个巨大的混凝土立方体中，比如说边10英尺。按钮和屏幕被布线到外部。所以如果你有个人识别码，它就会像预期的那样工作，只是有点笨拙。如果你想偷它，你必须挖出并拖走一大块混凝土，或者用手持锤去拿它中间的某个地方的电子产品。希望噪音能把邻居们吵醒。
我预计，几年后，随着超比特币化的临近，花旗银行(Citibank)或富国银行(Wells Fargo)或其他人将真正做到这一点。
在我开始探讨超比特币化和我们现在必须面对的尴尬的货币汤之前，您介意简要解释一下为什么黄金是货币的基础吗？
在这个日复一日的世界里，人们很容易忘记黄金，因为我更多地考虑的是我每GB 1美元能买到的那袋糖果，而不是什么让我的每GB 1美元有任何价值的原因。
金钱不是你通常想的东西，也不是大多数人想过的东西。钱只是你用的东西，就像电一样。只要你按下开关，灯就亮了，谁在乎电是怎么工作的。
只有当灯开始闪烁时，你才会开始在意。受过高中教育的阿根廷出租车司机比拥有博士学位的兰德智库经济学家更了解金钱的运作方式。因为“经济学人”的光芒从未熄灭过。如果出租车司机超过30岁，他已经让他们出去两三次了。
如果你注意了，灯开始闪烁了，对每个人来说都是如此。
那么什么是钱呢？我们想要的钱是什么？为什么黄金是这一点的基础呢？
这上面洒了很多墨水。我想该轮到我了。开始了！
1，我们只想让钱发挥作用。应该很方便。货币便利性意味着稀缺性、耐用性、可分割性和可互换性(也称为可互换)。
2，我们希望它能够工作，而不必信任太多的系统或人员。钱应该是信任度最低的。
在Money…之前。我们实际上并不知道那么多。人类学家和考古学家对当时的社会如何经济运作做了大量的理论研究。不过幸运的是，其中至少有一部分是有记录的历史。
无论如何，如果你是比特币MAX，那么不管怎么说，流行的故事都是这样的。
早期部落的默认制度是礼物经济和荣誉制度。这些都很棒，在公社和小村庄里仍然有。但是他们需要很大的信任。当你没有信任的时候，钱就是你用的。
让我们试着以物易物吧。我给你一头牛，你给我一些小麦。不需要信任。即刻结算。但是不太方便。因为如果我想要的小麦数量与奶牛的价值不符怎么办？奶牛不是很容易区分的。我不想为一头整头(未分割的)奶牛吃太多小麦，因为我必须在它变坏之前把它吃了。小麦是相当可分的，但它不耐用。如果你让牛活着，它比小麦更耐用。而且，如果你把牛肉干，或者制作面粉，在一定条件下储存，牛和小麦都可以变得更耐用。但这是一项繁重的工作。
我们可以试着用债务。我给你一头牛，你给我一些小麦，你欠我一些小麦。如果我想从另一个人那里得到什么，而他也想要小麦，我可以让你把你欠我的小麦给他，然后我就能得到我想要的。但这需要信任。不只是你我之间，还有你和另一个人。钱的全部意义就是把信任降到最低。债务不是钱。它有它的用途。但这不是钱。
奶牛和小麦也是不可替代的。这是个花哨的词，意思是“可互换”。一种商品或商品的财产，其单个单位基本上是可互换的，其每个部分与另一个部分无法区分。有些小麦可能会有点变质。你不能用一头老牛代替一头新牛。
我们使用wampum，也就是海贝壳要好得多。蛤壳稀缺，可替换，经久耐用，足够小，你不需要将它们分开，而且很难假冒。你也可以把它们戴在项链上，便于携带。虽然有些稀缺，但也不算太稀缺。海滩上有足够多的蛤蜊壳，你可以在它们上面运行经济。比牛和小麦好多了。
如果你有足够的wampum，我就把牛卖给你，我们马上就算账。由于其良好的特性，wampum变得普遍接受。因此，如果我相信你会给我一张借条，那么很容易用wampum来计价债务。因此，它也让债务变得更容易管理。
至于我们想要的钱是什么，用wampum我们基本上已经完成了。
当你堆成大堆时，贝壳会被压扁。没有我们希望的那么耐用。而且有些贝壳比其他贝壳更漂亮，所以你最终会试图从wampum项链中挑选出最漂亮的贝壳，这会浪费时间。不像我们希望的那样具有互换性。此外，由于蛤蜊长在岩石上，而你一直在吃，如果你带来的蛤蜊比被碾碎或当作珠宝藏起来的更多，你就会得到蛤壳膨胀。并不像我们希望的那样稀缺。
黄金基本上是wampum，但更均匀耐用，供应有限。你把它熔化并提纯，所有的黄金都是一样的。它不生锈，也不会碎裂。而且它被很好地掩埋了，所以通货膨胀是有争议的。事实上，当发现一个新的大型金矿时，就会出现通货膨胀问题，就像在各种历史上的淘金热潮中发生的那样。但总体而言还不算太差。
这就是为什么当我们想到金钱时，从地下城、龙到迪士尼电影，我们集体脑海中浮现的是黄金。
尼克·萨博(Nick Szabo)在“支付”中谈到了从wampum到贵金属的转变，以及其他许多有趣的事情：货币的起源，这是从经典到比特币的座右铭。
顺便说一句，乔希(或其他读者)在考虑想要的便利属性--稀缺、耐用、可分割、可替换--时，你认为黄金有问题吗？如果有的话，你会说哪一个是最重要的？顺便说一句，没有正确的答案，只是需要考虑一下。
黄金不会生锈！这很有趣。这一点和你其余的回答在我的思维导图中构成了一个巨大的基础，那就是如何思考金钱。
因此，可分割性似乎就是金价下跌的地方。或许也是比特币站稳脚跟的地方。(除了可以通过互联网发送--你之前链接到的中正帖子，想象一下，一种贵金属可以通过通信渠道运输，现在更有意义了。)。
但在我们到达那里之前，为什么黄金不能完全从现代货币中提炼出来呢？我隐约知道，金本位制在七八十年代的某个时候被取消或放宽了……。这个世界似乎在艰难前行。
人们感觉，在空壳交易者的社区里，贝壳的涌入可能会造成很大的麻烦，但在现代发达经济体中，这似乎更不透明。我在海滩上找不到现代货币，所以我自己的个人供应很有限，宽松的金本位制对当今大多数人生活的世界有什么影响？
你可以试试。这不是我们第一次去牛仔竞技表演了。自罗马帝国以来，各国政府一直在膨胀并脱离金本位制。但黄金一直在回升。因为人们喜欢存黄金。因为他们理所当然地担心政府货币的通货膨胀会导致价值的急剧下降。大多数现代储户可能更喜欢房子、股票或艺术品。但黄金永远不会完全消失。它是受保护货币的象征。它在集体思想中占据了那个位置。
我在海滩上找不到现代钞票，所以我自己的个人供应量有限。
在你的个人经历中，这对你来说是正确的，但并不是所有人都是正确的。阿根廷出租车司机的情况并非如此。对于魏玛利亚德国人来说就不是这样了。对你来说，这不会无限期地持续下去。
恶性通货膨胀就像是破产。它在很长一段时间内发生得很慢。然后它一下子就发生了。我们正接近快慢阶段之间的拐点。你可以看看各大中央政府的资产负债表，看到政府债务数额的巨大增长，这转化为印钞。
据我所知，自CORONA以来，美国联邦储备委员会(Federal Reserve)的资产负债表翻了一番，从大约3万亿美元增加到6万亿美元。这在世界各地都是相似的。这些数字让人目瞪口呆。2008年，这一数字较小，但仍令人难以置信。每一次危机，数字都会变得更大。在某种程度上，你会给小费。
沙滩上有更多的贝壳等着你。当你感觉到的时候，总有一天会为你而来。
然后每个人都是百万富翁，但是货架上没有牛奶。政府法定货币的恶性通货膨胀是一件可怕的事情。它是种族灭绝、世界大战和几代人挥之不去的创伤的产物。这种痛苦和恐惧就是为什么储蓄者自从有了政府的钱就一直渴望黄金的原因。如果你正在经历法定货币贬值的缓慢阶段，保护你的购买力听起来很无聊，但在你给了小费之后，这真的是生死攸关。
对于那些较晚采用比特币的人来说，超比特币不会是一件令人愉快的事情，尤其是在情感上。但与恶性通货膨胀相比，这是一个好得多的选择。不管怎样，大多数人的大部分储蓄都不是以法定形式存在的。大多数人都是债务人。菲亚特的债务人。随着超比特币化的进行，对越来越多怨恨的千禧一代来说，感觉像债务奴役的住房抵押贷款和学生贷款将以贬值的、最终一文不值的法定货币计价。这相当于一个禧年，你基本上只是注销了所有无论如何都不会偿还的债务。
我的直觉是，超比特币化会让人感觉有点像俄罗斯共产主义的垮台。这是一段艰难的时期，但总体上是朝着正确的方向前进的。你会注意到，你的电费账单希望你用比特币支付，711希望你用比特币支付冰沙，然后逐渐首先，然后是所有地方，所有人都只想用比特币支付。然后事情就结束了，你再也不会去想这件事了。
不知何故，发达经济体(在很长一段时间内，肯定是在我的记忆中)避免了恶性通货膨胀……。但是它马上就要来了！当它到来的时候，比特币就坐在那里等着吃掉/解决所有的混乱。
可以说，你的头寸与价值投资者沃伦·巴菲特(Warren Buffet)的头寸相似。长期以来，他一直觉得市场价格过高，以至于他拥有超过1300亿美元的现金储备，他没有信心以今天的价格进行投资。但他相信以价值为基础的定价的基本面，以至于等待是一种显而易见的策略-尽管在此期间，他错过了许多有争议的虚假价值。
你有没有同样的信念，认为发达经济体的恶性通货膨胀/超级比特币化是一个如此根本的事件，这只是一个时间问题，而不是是否会发生的问题？
巴菲特公开表示不喜欢比特币。对他来说，股票和企业是一种价值储存。他明白他们的意思，而且他做功课。沃伦也不投资科技股。技术并不是他的优势所在。
对于比特币持有者来说，比特币是他们的优势所在。他们已经做了功课。不只是在经济方面，所有不方便和令人厌恶的科技东西也是如此。比如学会使用冷钱包，保护备份的安全，而不是把硬币放在容易蒸发的交易所里，所有这些都是如此。如果我没记错的话，你在这里被咬了。这很常见。我也有过。很多比特币只是在痛苦中学习。
比特币狂热者是狂热分子，但他们是理性狂热分子。这就是为什么你会从早期得到这些巨大的冰冷硬币的原因，这些硬币从来都卖不出去。这是“指环王”里的咕噜。或者是斯茂格。
因此，无论如何，我和巴菲特在比特币作为价值投资的问题上意见不一--因为巴菲特还没有做好比特币的功课--但我们在价值投资的大前提上意见一致。关于巴菲特投资的那些东西，我也没有做过功课，所以我不会轻蔑。作为一名投资者，很多成功都是专业的，知道你知道什么和不知道什么，并且足够聪明地坚持自己的路线。
就目前而言，美元是一种很好的价值储存手段，所以巴菲特持有他的美元储备是不错的，尽管他可能会错过下一波比特币价格升值的机会。当他需要上船的时候，他会把它弄清楚的。
是的，我对比特币深信不疑。当然，没有什么是百分之百的。但我的想法是，在12到15年内实现超比特币化，届时我们将稳定在每枚比特币1000万美元左右的购买力。
抱歉，我只是想澄清一下，我并不是说你们持有相同的观点，而是你们都对一些你们认为几乎是基本的东西有强烈的信念。
尽管我听说比特币是由一位名叫智史(Satoshi)的人创立的，但我无法想象，如果是一个有想法的人与网络上的其他人隔绝开来，比特币还会产生如此大的吸引力。感觉就像，尽管它归功于一个人，但它一定是从一个对共同主题感兴趣的在线社区中产生的。
是这样吗？如果是这样的话，他们是在讨论比特币解决的浮动法定问题，还是比特币的存在是为了解决另一个问题？发现自己是另一个人的解决方案。
我有点像是在问一个问题，想看看比特币的大爆炸。不是后来的扩张，而是理解它产生的背后的想法，并描绘它发生的那一刻。
我个人的中本聪超级英雄起源故事是，他是一个毛茸茸的人。也就是说，喜欢穿着动物主题连体裙来表现性取向的人。
让我们直截了当地谈吧。不管你是不是毛茸茸的，当你发送一张Dick照片时，想法是只有你和你在交流渠道另一端的特殊女性朋友才能享受它。如果你用邮政服务寄出你的照片，这基本上是真的。即使邮局打开了你的邮件，至少你可能会注意到信封看起来被篡改了。但。
.
note_en:
It is the background image for  your cryptocurrency focused blog, for your  Twitter bio, and we have chosen it as the image to represent this Taaalk.
 What does this image mean to you and how does it relate to your thoughts on bitcoin?
  JMG is not a bitcoin guy, more of a gold bug if anything. But his idea that we have peaked technologically and that the future looks more like the past than some star trek utopia, or blade runner dystopia, resonates with me. To me what bitcoin represents most of all is a return to the gold standard. Except it will be a bitcoin standard. Because bitcoin is a bit more convenient to carry around and transact in.
 As mircea popescu put it in  Bitcoin and the Poor, "Bitcoin is the most conservative thing since at least queen Victoria, if not outright Jesus. Bitcoin makes so-called "progressive" tax schemes unworkable. Bitcoin makes any sort of public welfare untenable. Bitcoin makes anyone's pretense of equality with anyone else risible. Bitcoin isn't here to "make communes work", but quite the contrary : it will render communes both inoperable and uninteresting to pretty much everyone."
 I'm not quite as dark enlightenment trolly as popescu, but there's a lot there that I agree with. Bitcoin is not some futuristic kumbaya sit around the campfire thing. It's a return of nature, of limits, of traditional values, and to a large degree it may feel like a reset to earlier times.
 I think some countries may be able to retain some vestiges of the modern welfare state, post hyperbitcoinization. It won't be easy. There is not going to be a universal basic income (UBI). Sorry.
  Firstly, it seems bitcoin is instrumental to the development of a future like the one described above. This  not obvious to me. Is this correct? And if so why is this the case?
  Under a gold standard, there is a limit to what the government can do to your money. Say the ruling class wants to start a war, or build a new stadium for your local community college. They can tax. Or they can borrow. If they borrow, they have to pay back what they borrowed in gold, like the  Iron Bank in Game of Thrones.
 And if gold actually worked that way, a gold standard would actually set some limits.
  Actually who needs a gold "standard" -- we could just transact in gold for everything, like god intended. No government currencies needed.
 We got government currencies because gold does not in fact act like our platonic ideal of it.
 1, Gold doesn't work for for small transactions. Even if the 711 liquor store wanted to accept gold, you couldn't buy a slurpee with it. You can't transact in gold dust. Because it blows away and gets lost.
 2, gold is surprisingly easy to counterfeit. Lot of gold painted tungsten bars on ebay. This is good enough to pass a water volume test. Do you have an x ray crystallographer?
  There's a bunch more properties where bitcoin wins. But I think even without 3, if we just had 1 and 2, we might have a ubiquitous gold transaction world like in fantasy novels.
 But since gold is easy to counterfeit, we need coins stamped by the government, with government police to punish counterfeiters. You have to pay the police. It's expensive.
 And since gold doesn't work with small transactions, we have small denomination coins and paper notes for that.
 Let's add 4, maybe the most important one. Gold is hard to secure. So we keep it in the place we trust the most. The government bank. And we use paper that is redeemable for gold. When the ruling class then wants a war it is just too tempting for their lackeys in the government to say, sorry, your pesos that used to be worth 1/16 ounce of gold, are now worth 1/23 ounce. But don't worry about it. The economy needed it.  Nixon.  Roosevelt.  Germany.  England.  The roman empire. Everyone did it. So it's not like people didn't see it coming after it happened all these times. But gold is just too hard to secure, there was really no better option.
 You could argue war mongering lackeys is just a straw man. Well intentioned democratically elected leaders that want to promote social equality are even worse! Everyone wants to spend, spend spend, whether you agree with their ideals or not.
 Getting back to point 4, secure storage. In  Silas Marner, this weaver works his whole life to save up a gold hoard and hides it in the floor of his little hut. Then this dissolute upper class guy figures out where he hid it and just takes it to pay off a gambling debt.
 Not so easy with bitcoin! Actually not so hard. If Dunstan had gotten Silas's hardware wallet paper backup and Silas wasn't using a passphrase, he could have gotten it all. But Silas CAN use a passphrase. Silas can use 2-of-2 or 2-of-3 multisig for deep cold storage, and keep the other keys with people he trusts. Or more realistically, since he has trust issues, at least somewhere harder to get at than the floor of his hut.
  People say, bitcoin is too hard to use, nobody can go through all this hassle to secure a hardware wallet. Fine, have your bank secure it. The point is, bitcoin gives you a choice.
 The issue with gold is, because of its physical properties you have no choice but to trust third party vaults. Once you are doing that, the best option is to trust the government central bank vault. And the government can't be trusted not to devalue.
 Bitcoin works the way gold bugs imagine gold works. To my mind this is why you need bitcoin to get a real gold standard. Or rather a  bitcoin standard.
 PS: A great way to secure a hardware wallet is to embed it in a giant cube of concrete, say 10 feet on a side. The buttons and screen get routed to the outside . So if you have the pin code, it works as expected, just kind of unwieldy. If you want to steal it, you have to dig out and haul a huge block of concrete, or use jackhammers to get at the electronics somewhere in the middle of it. Hopefully the noise would wake up the neighbors.
 I expect in a few years, with hyperbitcoinization approaching, citibank or wells fargo or someone will actually be doing this.
  Before I get onto exploring hyper bitcoinization and the awkward monetary soup we must now be in, would you mind briefly explaining why gold is fundamental to money?
 It is easy to forget about gold in a day-to-day world where I'm thinking more about the bag of sweets my $/£ is going to get me, than what is responsible for my $/£ having any value at all.
  Money isn't something that you usually think about, or most people ever think about. Money is just something you use, like electricity. As long as the lights go on when you flip the switch, who cares how electricity works.
 It's when the lights start flickering that you start to care. An argentine cab driver with a high school education is more sophisticated about how money works than a RAND thinktank economist with a phd. Because the lights haven't ever gone out for the economist. The cab driver if he's over 30 has had them go out already two or three times.
 If you are paying attention,  the lights are starting to flicker just a bit, for everyone.
 So what is money? What do we want money to be? And why is gold fundamental to that?
 There's a lot of ink been spilled on this. I guess it’s my turn. Here goes!
 1, we want money to just work. It should be CONVENIENT. Convenience for money means SCARCE, DURABLE, DIVISIBLE, and FUNGIBLE (aka interchangeable).
 2, we want it to work, without having to trust too many systems or people. Money should be TRUST MINIMIZING.
 Before money… we don’t actually know that much. Anthropologists and archaeologists do a lot of theorizing about how societies worked economically back in the day.  Luckily though, at least some of this is recorded history.
 At any rate the prevailing story, if you are a bitcoin maxi anyway,  goes something like this.
 The default system for tribes of the early days is gift economies and honor systems. These are great, and you still have them in communes and small villages. But they require a lot of TRUST. Money is what you use when you don't have trust.
 Let's try bartering. I give you a cow, you give me some wheat. No trust required. Instant settlement. But not very convenient. Because what if the amount of wheat I want doesn’t match the value of the cow? Cows aren't very DIVISIBLE. And I don’t want to accept too much wheat for a whole (undivided) cow, because I would have to eat it before it goes bad. Wheat is fairly divisible but it isn't DURABLE. A cow is more durable than wheat, if you keep it alive. And both cows and wheat can be made more durable if you jerky the cow, or make flour and store it under certain conditions. But it's a lot of work.
 We could try using debt. I give you a cow, you give me some wheat, and you owe me some wheat for later. If there’s something I want from another guy and he also wants wheat, I can get you to give him the wheat you owe me and then I get what I want. But this requires TRUST. Not just between you and me, but you and the other guy. The whole point of money is to minimize trust. Debt isn't money. It has its uses. But it isn't money.
 Cows and wheat also aren't  FUNGIBLE. Which is a fancy word for interchangeable. "The property of a good or a commodity whose individual units are essentially interchangeable, and each of its parts is indistinguishable from another part." Some wheat could be a little spoiled. You can't substitute an old cow for a new cow.
 We're much better off using wampum, i.e. sea shells. Clam shells are scarce, fungible, durable, small enough you don't need to divide them, and hard to counterfeit. You can also wear them in necklaces, making them convenient to carry around. And while somewhat scarce, they aren't too scarce. There’s enough clam shells on the beach that you can run an economy on them. Much better than cows and wheat.
 If you have enough wampum, I just sell you the cow and we square up right away. Thanks to its good properties, wampum becomes universally acceptable. Therefore, if I trust you for an IOU, it’s easy to denominate the debt in wampum. So it makes debt more manageable too.
 As far as what we want money to be, with wampum we are basically done.
  Shells get squished when you make giant piles. Not as durable as we'd like. And some shells are prettier than other shells, so you wind up trying to pick out the nicest shells from the wampum necklaces, which wastes time. Not as fungible as we'd like.  Also since clams grow on rocks, and you are eating them all the time, you get clam shell inflation if you are bringing in more clams than what gets squished or squirreled away as jewelry. Not as scarce as we'd like.
 Gold is basically wampum, but more uniform and durable, and limited in supply. You melt it down and purify it, all gold is the same. It doesn’t rust, it doesn’t smush. And it’s pretty well buried, so inflation is wrangled. Actually there are inflation issues when a big new gold deposit gets discovered, like what happened in the various historic gold rushes. But overall not too bad.
  And that’s why when we think about money, from dungeons and dragons to disney movies, what pops up in our collective minds is gold.
 Nick Szabo talks about the transition from wampum to precious metals, and many other interesting things, in  Shelling Out: The Origins of Money, which is canon to bitcoin maxis.
 As an aside to Josh (or other readers) when considering the desired convenience properties -- SCARCE, DURABLE, DIVISIBLE, FUNGIBLE -- are you seeing issues with gold? Which, if any, would you say is the most significant? No right answer btw, just something to think about.
  Gold doesn't rust! That is interesting. That and the rest of your answer built a huge fundamental building block in my 'how to think about money' mind map.
 So  divisibility seems to be where gold falls down. And perhaps where Bitcoin stands up. (As well as being able to be sent through the internet - satoshi's post you linked to earlier,  imagine a precious metal that "can be transported over a communications channel", is making more sense now.)
 But before we get there, why can't gold be totally abstracted from modern money? I know vaguely that the gold standard was removed or relaxed some time in the 70s/80s... and the world seems to have trundled on.
 It feels in a community of shell traders, an influx of shells could cause a great deal of trouble, but in a modern developed economy it seems more opaque. I can't find modern money on the beach, so my own personal supply is constrained, how does a relaxing gold standard impact the world most people live in today?
  You can try. This isn't our first trip to the rodeo. Governments have been inflating and going off the gold standard since the roman empire. But gold keeps coming back. Because people like saving in gold. Because they are rightfully afraid of the grinding value decay of inflation in government money. The majority of modern savers may prefer houses, or stocks, or art. But gold never quite goes away. It is the symbol of protected money. It occupies that spot in the collective mind.
 "I can't find modern money on the beach, so my own personal supply is constrained"
 That has been true for you, in your personal experience, but it is not universally true. It is not true for the Argentine cab driver. It was not true for Weimarian germans. It will not continue to be true for you, indefinitely.
 Hyperinflation is like bankruptcy. It happens slowly over a long period of time. And then it happens all at once. We are getting near the inflection point between the slow and fast phases. You can look at the balance sheets of the various big central governments and see an enormous rise in the amount of government debt, which translates to money printing.
 From what I can recall, US federal reserve balance sheet doubled since corona, from something like  3 Trillion to 6 Trillion USD . It's similar across the world. The numbers boggle the mind. In 2008 the numbers were smaller but still mind boggling. Each crisis the numbers get bigger. At some point, you tip.
 More shells on the beach for you. There will come a day for you when you feel it.
 And then everyone is a millionaire, but there is no milk on the shelves.  A government fiat money hyperinflation is a terrifying thing to live through. It's the stuff of genocide, world war, and generations of lingering trauma. This pain and fear is why savers have craved gold ever since there was government money. Protecting your purchasing power sounds boring if you are living through the slow phase of fiat money decay, but after you tip, it is literally life and death.
   Hyperbitcoinization won't be pleasant, especially emotionally, for those who are late adopting bitcoin. But it is a far better option than hyperinflation. Most people don't have the bulk of their savings in fiat anyway. Most people are debtors. Fiat debtors. With hyperbitcoinization in play, the home mortgages and student loans that feel like debt slavery to an increasing number of resentful millenials will be denominated in devalued and eventually worthless fiat. It amounts to a  jubilee, where you essentially just write off all the debts that were never going to be paid anyway.
 My gut feeling is that hyperbitcoinization will feel a bit like the fall of communism in russia. A rough time, but moving in the right direction overall.  You will notice that your electric bill wants you to pay in bitcoin, and the 711 wants you to pay for your slurpee in bitcoin, and gradually first then everywhere all at once everyone just wants to be paid in bitcoin. And then it is done, and you don't think about it anymore.
  Somehow the 'developed' economies have (for a long time, certainly in my living memory) avoided hyperinflation... But it's a'coming! And when it comes, Bitcoin is sitting there waiting to eat up/resolve all the chaos.
 One could say you have a similar position to that of Warren Buffet ( the value investor). He has felt markets have been overpriced for so long that he has a $130Bn+ cash pile he is not confident to invest at today's prices. But he believes in the fundamentals of 'value' based pricing to such an extent that waiting is an obvious strategy - even though he has missed out on a lot of debatably 'faux value' during that time.
 Do you have the same faith that developed-economy-hyperinflation / hyperbitcoinization is such a fundamental event that it is a matter of when, rather than if?
  Buffett is on the record as disliking bitcoin. For him, stocks and businesses are a store of value. He understands them, and he does his homework. Warren doesn't invest in tech stocks either. Tech is not where he has an edge.
 For bitcoin hodlers, bitcoin is where they have an edge. They have done their homework. Not just on the economics story, but all the inconvenient and aversive tech stuff as well.  Like learning to use cold wallets, keeping backups safe, not keeping coins on exchanges where they have a tendency to evaporate, all of that. You got bitten here if I recall correctly. That's common. I have as well. A lot of bitcoin is just learning through pain.
 Bitcoin hodlers are fanatics, but they are rational fanatics. That's why you get these giant hoards of deep cold coins from the early days that just never sell. It's gollum from lord of the rings. Or smaug.
 So anyway, buffett and I disagree on bitcoin as a value investment -- because buffett hasn't done his homework on bitcoin -- but we agree on the general premise of value investing. I haven't done my homework on the kinds of stuff buffett invests in either, so no scorn from me. A lot of success as an investor is specializing, knowing what you know and don't know, and being smart enough to stay in your lane.
 For now the dollar is a good store of value, so buffett is fine holding onto his dollar hoard, though he may miss out on the next wave of bitcoin price appreciation. When he needs to get on board he will get it wrangled.
 Yes, I am convinced on bitcoin. Of course nothing is 100%. But my thinking is hyperbitcoinization in 12 to 15 years, at which point we stabilize at around $10m of purchasing power per bitcoin.
  Sorry, just to be clear I wasn't saying that you held the same perspectives - but that you both have strong convictions about something which you view to be almost fundamental.
  Even though I have heard that one individual named 'satoshi' started bitcoin, I can't imagine it would have developed the traction it did if it was one person with an idea who was isolated form the rest of the web. It feels like, even though it is credited to one person, it must have emerged from an online community interested in a common theme.
 Is that the case? And if so were they discussing the floaty-fiat problem bitcoin solves, or did bitcoin exist to solve a different problem? And find itself to be the solution to another.
 I'm sort of asking a question to try to see the "big bang" of bitcoin. Not the expansion that followed afterwards, but to understand the ideas behind its spawning into existence and to picture the moment it happened.
  My personal satoshi nakamoto superhero origin story is, he was a furry. That is, someone who prefers to express his sexuality wearing an animal-themed onesie.
 Let’s get right down to it. Furry or not, when you send a dick pic, the idea is that only you and your special lady friend at the other end of the communications channel should be able to enjoy it. This would be basically true if you sent your pics with the postal service. Even if the post office opens your mail, at least you might notice the envelope looks tampered with. But

note_en:
A leaker today posted on Twitter a link to a file sharing service that contains what an anonymous source claims is a portion of Intel's crown jewels: A 20GB folder of confidential Intel intellectual property. The leaker dubbed the release the "Intel exconfidential Lake Platform Release ;)."
 The folder has been posted by an anonymous source that claims more is coming soon, and while we don't know the exact specifics of the folder's contents, we have verified that it does exist. In fact, the title of many of the documents do correlate to the list of purported information posted by the leaker:
 Kabylake (Purley Platform) BIOS Reference Code and Sample Code + Initialization code (some of it as exported git repos with full history)
   The anonymous leaker claims the hacker "breached" Intel and the files were obtained earlier this year, adding "most of the things here have NOT been published ANYWHERE before and are classified as confidential, under NDA or Intel Restricted Secret." The leaker says more files will be shared soon, and "the future parts of this leak will have even juicier and more classified stuff."
 Interestingly, the tweeter also notes "If you find password protected zips in the release the password is probably either "Intel123" or "intel123". This was not set by me or my source, this is how it was acquired from Intel."
 The poster encourages downloaders to look for mentions of 'backdoors' in some of the Intel source code, and even provides a sample clip of one such listing, but we aren't sure of the intentions behind the listings in the code.
 We've reached out to Intel for comment on the matter, and the company tells us it is investigating the situation. We expect further comment soon.
 The link is being widely distributed on Twitter, but it might be best to exercise caution – downloading any file from an untrusted source is always a risk.

note:
使用微内核的好处是安全性--它的代码足够少，您可以确信它是完全安全的。
缺点是，因为在其他设计下原本驻留在内核中的东西现在都在用户区域，所以它们必须执行IPC来通信--而IPC有一些开销。
IPC是微内核中最热的热路径，相应地，任天堂将IPC中涉及的每个函数都标记为__ATTRIBUTE__((ALYSY_INLINE))，这对逆向工程来说是一种巨大的痛苦。
此外，任天堂将SvcReplyAndReceive实现为允许微服务服务器进程在一次调用中回复和接收新消息的单个系统调用。
这就是说，实际上管理费用比你想象的要少。FUSE之所以比用于FS的内核驱动程序慢，过去是因为FUSE必须与内核对话才能执行文件系统操作，所以当您读取文件时，您的进程-&>FUSE-&>内核-&>硬件。相比之下，在Horizon上，内核完全不参与文件系统管理(它甚至没有映射sdmmc硬件)。因此，进程将执行进程-&gt；FS系统模块进程-&gt；硬件。
在Horizon中，所有东西显然都不是文件。没有Unix/Linux有特殊的/dev/东西那样的全局文件系统路径。
Horizon中不存在管道--所有IPC都是通过Horizon IPC(HIPC)协议完成的。
UNIX/POSIX有fork()和子进程之类的东西……但是在基于功能的操作系统中，创建进程是一项令人难以置信的特权操作。Fork()不可能在Horizon中实现，所有线程都是通过SvcCreateThread()创建的。子进程并不存在。
note_en:
The advantage to using a microkernel is security -- there's sufficiently little code that you can actually be confident it's all secure.
 The disadvantage is that because things that would live in the kernel under other designs are now in userland, they have to do IPC to communicate -- and IPC has some overhead.
 IPC is the hottest hot-path in a microkernel, correspondingly Nintendo marked every function involved in IPC as __attribute__((always_inline)), this was kind of a huge pain to reverse engineer as a result.
 In addition, Nintendo implemented "SvcReplyAndReceive" as a single system call that allows a microservice server process to reply to and receive a new message in one invocation.
 That said, there's actually less overhead than you think. Past of why FUSE is slower than a kernel driver for FS is because FUSE has to talk to the kernel to do filesystem stuff, so when you read a file you have your process -> FUSE -> kernel -> hardware. In comparison, on Horizon the kernel is completely uninvolved in filesystem management (it doesn't even have the sdmmc hardware mapped). Thus processes will do process -> FS system module process -> hardware.
    In Horizon, everything is very distinctly not a file. There's no global filesystem paths the way that unix/linux have special  /dev/whatever.
 Pipes don't exist in Horizon -- all IPC is done via the  horizon ipc ("HIPC") protocol.
 UNIX/POSIX have stuff like fork() and child processes...but creating a process is an incredibly privileged operation in a capability-based operating system. Fork() is impossible to implement in Horizon, all threads are created via SvcCreateThread() instead. Child processes aren't a thing that exist.

周二，黎巴嫩首都贝鲁特一个港口发生爆炸，造成建筑物受损，100多人死亡，数千人受伤。人们录制的视频显示了冲击波和高耸的红色云，有些人将其与核武器的蘑菇云相提并论。
爆炸的确切原因尚不清楚，但调查的重点是一个仓库不当储存了2750吨硝酸铵。
爆炸记录为3.3级地震，爆炸的影响在距离爆炸地点几英里远的地方都能感觉到。专家告诉Insider，爆炸的爆炸当量很可能是数百吨TNT当量。
--Nader Itayim|‌‌نادرایتیّم(@ncitayim)2020年8月4日。
加州米德尔伯里国际问题研究所的核武器和常规武器专家杰弗里·刘易斯(Jeffrey Lewis)根据爆炸破坏、冲击波、地震信号和陨石坑的大小，估计当量在200吨到500吨之间。
这一爆炸威力至少是GBU-43/B大型军械空中爆炸(绰号“炸弹之母”)的近20倍，后者的爆炸当量约为11吨，是美国武器库中威力最大的非核武器。
这种武器最早是在2017年4月在阿富汗打击伊斯兰国的战斗中使用的。
贝鲁特的爆炸威力如此之大，以至于一些观察人士担心这座城市经历了某种形式的核爆炸，爆炸后高耸在爆炸现场上空的蘑菇云加剧了这种恐惧。
贝鲁特州长将周二可怕的爆炸与二战期间摧毁日本城市广岛和长崎的原子弹相提并论。
由于爆炸当量为几百吨，贝鲁特爆炸的威力将明显低于摧毁广岛的原子弹，后者的估计当量约为15000吨。
然而，爆炸威力将与当量最低的B61核重力弹相当，据信B61核重力弹的爆炸当量约为300吨。
一些专家估计贝鲁特事件的爆炸当量为1至2千吨，这将使爆炸比一些较小的美国战术核武器威力更大。
比较到此为止，美国科学家联合会的核武器专家汉斯·克里斯滕森(Hans Kristensen)在谈到将贝鲁特爆炸比作核爆炸时说。他指出，Insider的非官方爆炸当量估计为几百吨。
他说，压力波会快得多，因为不受控制的核连锁反应释放的能量比化学爆炸释放的能量快得多。他进一步解释说，核爆炸也会产生强烈的辐射，包括在地面引爆核武器产生的尘埃。
军控协会的裁军和减少威胁专家金斯顿·赖夫(Kingston Reif)告诉Insider，虽然贝鲁特爆炸的爆炸当量可能与美国一些核武器的爆炸当量相当，但这根本不意味着爆炸会让人感觉像是引爆了当量最低的B61变种。
他辩称，核爆炸会更糟糕，因为它将包括更极端的热效应和辐射效应，并补充说，核武器不仅仅是另一种武器，这是有原因的。
据黎巴嫩官员称，涉嫌引发大规模爆炸的硝酸铵是几年前从一艘货船上没收的。这场爆炸造成了数英里的大范围破坏，造成100多人死亡，多达5000人受伤。
这种物质是一种普通的肥料，是一种高度爆炸性的材料，曾发生过多次毁灭性的爆炸。
例如，2015年中国天津发生的大规模爆炸，造成包括99名消防员在内的160多人死亡，300多座建筑受损，部分原因是800吨硝酸铵；1947年德克萨斯州得克萨斯州发生的爆炸，造成500多人死亡，涉及2300吨硝酸铵的引爆。
贝鲁特的破坏被认为已经导致30多万人无家可归，而这个国家在经济和政治上都在苦苦挣扎。
